channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Arizona AG files criminal charges against Kalshi over 'illegal gambling'
item.description: Arizona is the first state to allege the prediction market company has committed criminal violations, accusing it of running an unlicensed gambling operation.
item.content:encoded:
Arizona is the first state to allege the prediction market company has committed criminal violations, accusing it of running an unlicensed gambling operation.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: USS Ford has seen war, fire and plumbing woes as it nears a record long deployment
item.description: The Ford's crew left Norfolk, Va., on June 24, initially bound for the Mediterranean. More than nine months later, the crew is now in the Red Sea for the war with Iran with no clear return date.
item.content:encoded:
The Ford's crew left Norfolk, Va., on June 24, initially bound for the Mediterranean. More than nine months later, the crew is now in the Red Sea for the war with Iran with no clear return date.

(Image credit: Jonathan Klein)
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: Living in boats: A postcard from Galveston County
item.description: More people are choosing to live aboard their boats, not only as a unique lifestyle choice in coastal cities, but also many are concerned about affording paying for a home or an apartment.
item.content:encoded: More people are choosing to live aboard their boats, not only as a unique lifestyle choice in coastal cities, but also many are concerned about affording paying for a home or an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Washburn

item.title: Travelers around the U.s. are hit with weather, airline and security delays
item.description: Travelers around the country are being hit with weather, airline and security delays.
item.content:encoded: Travelers around the country are being hit with weather, airline and security delays.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel J. Sánchez

item.title: CEOs urge Congress to end the government shutdown
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly spoke to Christopher Sununu, president and CEO of the industry group Airlines for America, about how a partial government shutdown is affecting tens of thousands of TSA agents.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly spoke to Christopher Sununu, president and CEO of the industry group Airlines for America, about how a partial government shutdown is affecting tens of thousands of TSA agents.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: Mamdani's public observance of Ramadan highlights Islam in NYC
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is working to normalize Muslim life in New York City. He faces headwinds from critics in the Jewish community as well as far-right activists and a recent attempted bomb attack.
item.content:encoded: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is working to normalize Muslim life in New York City. He faces headwinds from critics in the Jewish community as well as far-right activists and a recent attempted bomb attack.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: Khamenei's killing renews questions about U.S. assassinating foreign leaders
item.description: Technology allowed the U.S. and Israel to kill Iran's Supreme Leader, but raised longstanding questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be assassinating foreign leaders.
item.content:encoded:
Technology allowed the U.S. and Israel to kill Iran's Supreme Leader, but raised longstanding questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be assassinating foreign leaders.

(Image credit: Ahmad Al-Rubaye)
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: She's won 24 Paralympic medals. But Oksana Masters wants to talk about times she lost
item.description: Oksana Masters leaves Italy with five new para Nordic skiing medals, extending her reign as the most decorated U.S. Winter Paralympian. She competes in summer sports too and is already eyeing LA 2028.
item.content:encoded:
Oksana Masters leaves Italy with five new para Nordic skiing medals, extending her reign as the most decorated U.S. Winter Paralympian. She competes in summer sports too and is already eyeing LA 2028.

(Image credit: Buda Mendes)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Joe Kent, a top counterterrorism official, resigns citing Iran war
item.description: Kent said he "cannot in good conscience" back the Iran war. In his resignation letter, he says Iran "posed no imminent threat to our nation."
item.content:encoded:
Kent said he "cannot in good conscience" back the Iran war. In his resignation letter, he says Iran "posed no imminent threat to our nation."

(Image credit: Nathan Howard)
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk

item.title: New York City mayor celebrates Ramadan with inmates at Rikers Island
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani visited Rikers Island Monday to observe Iftar, or the breaking of the fast, with inmates. It's part of his efforts to reframe Muslim life in the city.
item.content:encoded: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani visited Rikers Island Monday to observe Iftar, or the breaking of the fast, with inmates. It's part of his efforts to reframe Muslim life in the city.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann
